title: Remove ReservedAnnotations from kube generate specification
body:
Reserved annotations are used internally by Podman and would effect nothing when run with Kubernetes so we should not be generating these annotations.

Fixes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/17105

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
Builtin Annotations will no longer show up in podman kube generate
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Wouldn't hurt to add a quick check that one of the the annotations (the CRI-O TTY one, for example?) doesn't exist in generated output to one of the existing `generate kube` tests, though
Yes I was hoping there were existing checks for this, but I can not find them now.

I went through the code and removed the ones that do not seem to be used related to CRI-O.
SandboxID seems to be used by checkpoint/restore, so left that one.

Please also add the diff:
```diff
diff --git a/test/system/710-kube.bats b/test/system/710-kube.bats                            
index 945d8d735c47..eb6bd6909a8b 100644                                                       
--- a/test/system/710-kube.bats                                                               
+++ b/test/system/710-kube.bats                                                               
@@ -33,6 +33,7 @@ json.dump(yaml.safe_load(sys.stdin), sys.stdout)'                           
     cname=c$(random_string 15)                                                               
     run_podman container create --cap-drop fowner --cap-drop setfcap --name $cname $IMAGE top
     run_podman kube generate $cname                                                          
+    assert "$output" !~ "Kubernetes only allows 63 characters"                               
                                                                                              
     # Convert yaml to json, and dump to stdout (to help in case of errors)                   
     json=$(yaml2json <<<"$output")                                                           
 ```

> I went through the code and removed the ones that do not seem to be used related to CRI-O.

I think we need to follow up (in another PR).  There are many annotations in `pkg/annotations` that we probably don't need anymore.
I agree but this would be an API break.

> I agree but this would be an API break.

What API would we break?
Anyone potentially using the Public interfaces in pkg/annotations

> Anyone potentially using the Public interfaces in pkg/annotations

I don't think that's part of the public API of Podman.  We only give promises to the bindings.  We never cared about these packages or promised it to be a Go library.

Lets get this in, and then we can remove the rest of those packages. I would just assume remove the pkg/annotations altogether. and use lib/annotations, for the ones we actually need.  Need to have @mheon look at these and figure which ones we need and whether we need it at all for suspend/resume.

LGTM
I'm gonna beat @vrothberg to the shiny button this time....
Thanks, @TomSweeneyRedHat!  My merge service isn't reachable over night :^)
And thanks for fixing the issue, @rhatdan!  Much nicer experience now, and I can record the tutorial :)
